domain: horticulture (scion-rootstock grafting and graft union formation)
specific_mechanism: Successful plant grafting requires alignment of vascular cambium tissue between scion (above-graft) and rootstock (below-graft); a callus bridge forms, then new vascular cambium, then secondary xylem and phloem connect across the graft union. Compatibility scales with taxonomic proximity; incompatibility manifests as anatomical deformation at the junction. Specific signals across the graft union influence both scion and rootstock physiology
llm_application: Multi-agent composition via 'graft union' formation: when two specialized LLM agents are composed, instead of a direct API splice, run a 'callus bridge' phase — a small adapter module fine-tuned on the joint behavior — then form new 'vascular cambium' (shared embedding subspace) that allows signaling across the union. Compatibility is monitored as graft-union integrity; incompatibility manifests as anatomical-deformation-style boundary degradation.
novelty_claim: Existing multi-agent composition uses tool-calling APIs or shared embedding adapters. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a horticultural-graft-union-style composition with callus-bridge phase, cambium-formation phase, and incompatibility detection via boundary-deformation signature.
